description: Phase 5 — AI: GitKraken Git AI parity + superset (PLAN.md §9; ADR-0008 BYOK + local Ollama, no backend; ADR-0009 explicit opt-in + redaction; ADR-0011 thin reqwest provider trait). Adds the lady-ai crate and provider abstraction, BYOK key management + consent + per-repo toggle, local Ollama + the remote providers, a context builder with token budgeting + secret redaction, then the user-facing AI features: commit messages, Commit Composer (logical split), explain, review-gated conflict resolution, PR/MR title/description + changelog + stash notes, and an MCP server exposing repo context. Post-release; builds on Phases 1-3 (partial-staging patch builder, 3-pane resolver) and Phase 4 (forge-agnostic hosting). Branches from main (Phase 4 complete + merged). Shared infra is already in the workspace from Phases 3-4 — reqwest/rustls, keyring, async-trait, tokio, and wiremock (dev) — so lady-ai mainly adds tiktoken-rs (budgeting), plus rmcp (PH5-011) and an optional vector store (PH5-012); run cargo-deny after adding each and allow-list any new permissive license as needed. Each story = one context-window commit. AI is opt-in and gated by explicit consent (ADR-0009); no remote call without consent; redaction tests are MANDATORY. Providers are tested against mocked HTTP (wiremock) with recorded responses — NEVER live APIs in CI (PLAN.md §10). UI stories rely on build + typecheck + unit tests with a manual window check noted.

userStories.title: lady-ai crate + AiProvider trait + task model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the AI abstraction so features are provider-agnostic.
acceptanceCriteria: New crate crates/lady-ai: #[async_trait] AiProvider { complete(req) -> AiResponse (streaming), id(), context_window() } over reqwest/rustls (ADR-0011); AiRequest/AiResponse + AiTask enum (CommitMessage|SplitCommits|Explain|ResolveConflict|PrTitle|PrDescription|Changelog|StashNote)
Provider registry + per-feature model selection config (serde, persisted to settings)
Streaming + cooperative cancellation supported in the trait shape
Unit tests with a fake in-crate provider; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green; cargo deny passes (reqwest/rustls permissive)
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §5.1, ADR-0011. Trait + types first; concrete providers in PH5-003/004. Workspace already provides reqwest/rustls + async-trait + tokio + wiremock (Phases 3-4); add tiktoken-rs in PH5-005. Stream to the UI via Tauri events (reuse the fetch/push-progress emitter pattern from Phase 2).

userStories.title: BYOK key management + consent + per-repo toggle
userStories.description: As a user, I control which provider Lady uses and whether my code leaves the machine.
acceptanceCriteria: Keys stored in the OS keychain via keyring (ADR-0008); never on disk plaintext, never logged
First-use consent flow (ADR-0009): the first action that would call a REMOTE provider shows which provider/model receives data and requires explicit opt-in; no silent default to cloud
Per-repo AI on/off toggle, default off until configured; ui settings to add/remove provider keys and pick the active provider
Unit tests: consent gate blocks a remote call until accepted; keychain abstraction get/set/delete (mocked); cargo fmt/clippy/test green; cargo deny passes; npm run build green
userStories.notes: ADR-0008 + ADR-0009. Trust-critical — never call remote without recorded consent. Reuse lady-hosting's TokenStore + KeyringStore (PH3-011/PH4-001) for keychain storage rather than a fresh impl; persist consent + the per-repo toggle + active-provider choice in settings (preserved like the license key, PH3-013).

userStories.title: Local Ollama provider (private path)
userStories.description: As a privacy-conscious user, I want AI that never leaves my machine.
acceptanceCriteria: AiProvider impl for a local Ollama endpoint (configurable host); model list + completion with streaming
Surfaced prominently as the 'never leaves your machine' option (ADR-0009); works with NO consent gate since it is local
Graceful message when Ollama is not running/installed; unit/integration test against a mocked Ollama HTTP endpoint; cargo fmt/clippy/test green; cargo deny passes
userStories.notes: ADR-0008 local-first path. Do the local provider before remote ones so the private default exists first.

userStories.title: Remote providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Azure, Mistral)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to bring keys for the major cloud providers.
acceptanceCriteria: AiProvider impls for OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, Azure OpenAI, and Mistral behind the PH5-001 trait, each with streaming + correct auth + endpoint shape
All remote calls go through the consent gate (PH5-002) and the redaction pass (PH5-005)
Unit tests per provider against mocked HTTP (wiremock) with recorded response fixtures; NO live API calls in tests; cargo fmt/clippy/test green; cargo deny passes
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §5.1, ADR-0008. One story across the five since the trait makes each a thin client; split if any one proves large.

userStories.title: Context builder + token budgeting + secret redaction
userStories.description: As a developer, I need quality, size-bounded, redacted context fed to the model.
acceptanceCriteria: lady-ai context builder: gather relevant diff/hunks + paths + branch + recent commit messages (style priming); detect Conventional Commits usage
Token budgeting with tiktoken-rs: rank/chunk hunks, map-reduce summarize large diffs, hard byte cap on payloads
MANDATORY secret-redaction pass (entropy + regex) before any REMOTE send; best-effort and documented as such (ADR-0009); prompts/responses with code not logged by default
Unit tests: redaction strips planted secrets; over-budget diffs are truncated/summarized deterministically; cargo fmt/clippy/test green; cargo deny passes
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §5.2, ADR-0009. Redaction tests are non-negotiable. Local Ollama may skip redaction but the pipeline still budgets.

userStories.title: AI commit message generation
userStories.description: As a user, I want a generated commit message for my staged changes.
acceptanceCriteria: An AiTask::CommitMessage flow: staged (or selected) diff -> context builder -> provider -> editable message preview; honors detected commit convention
ui: a 'Generate message' button in the commit box; streaming preview; user edits before committing; respects the per-repo AI toggle + consent
Unit test the prompt assembly + that a fake provider's output reaches the preview; cargo fmt/clippy/test green; cargo deny passes; npm run build green
userStories.notes: GitKraken parity: commit messages. Reuses Phase 2 staging.

userStories.title: Commit Composer (split into logical commits)
userStories.description: As a user, I want AI to organize my working changes into logical commits.
acceptanceCriteria: AiTask::SplitCommits: full working diff -> model returns a plan [{ message, hunk_ids }] (structured JSON, validated)
Apply the plan by staging each group via the Phase 2 partial-staging patch builder, then committing each group with its message; review/confirm before applying (never auto-commit silently)
ui: a Composer view showing proposed commits + their hunks, editable, with apply/cancel
Unit test: a known multi-change working tree yields a plan that, applied, produces the expected N commits; cargo fmt/clippy/test green; cargo deny passes; npm run build green
userStories.notes: GitKraken Commit Composer — the marquee AI feature. Depends on Phase 2 partial-staging (PLAN.md §5.3).

userStories.title: Explain (commit / branch / stash / working changes)
userStories.description: As a user, I want plain-English explanations of changes.
acceptanceCriteria: AiTask::Explain over a target (commit | branch range | stash | working changes); read-only; a 'Regenerate' re-rolls at higher temperature
ui: an 'Explain' action in commit details, branch context menu, stash list, and the Changes view; streaming output panel
Unit test prompt assembly per target + fake-provider round-trip; cargo fmt/clippy/test green; cargo deny passes; npm run build green

userStories.title: AI conflict resolution (review-gated)
userStories.description: As a user, I want AI suggestions to resolve merge conflicts, which I review.
acceptanceCriteria: AiTask::ResolveConflict: per conflict region feed base+ours+theirs+context -> model returns a proposed resolution
The proposal is shown in the Phase 3 3-pane resolver for the user to accept/edit; NEVER auto-written without explicit confirmation (ADR-0009 spirit + PLAN.md §5.3)
ui: an 'Auto-resolve with AI' action per conflicted file that populates the result pane as a suggestion
Unit test: a conflict + fake provider yields a suggestion that lands in the resolver model but is not auto-committed; cargo fmt/clippy/test green; cargo deny passes; npm run build green
userStories.notes: GitKraken parity: Auto-resolve with AI. Reuses Phase 3 PH3-001/002 resolver. Always review-gated.

userStories.title: AI PR title/description, changelog, stash notes
userStories.description: As a user, I want generated PR text, changelogs, and stash notes.
acceptanceCriteria: AiTask flows for PrTitle/PrDescription (summarize a branch's commit range), Changelog (group a range by Conventional-Commit type), and StashNote (summarize working changes when stashing)
ui: 'Generate' buttons in the Create-PR form (Phase 3/4), a changelog tool, and the stash dialog; editable output
Unit tests for each flow's prompt + fake-provider output; cargo fmt/clippy/test green; cargo deny passes; npm run build green
userStories.notes: GitKraken parity: PR titles, changelogs, stash notes. Hooks into Phase 3/4 PR creation + Phase 2 stash. PR creation is forge-agnostic since PH4-001 (PR vs MR label by remote); the generated text hooks the existing Refs create-PR form and must respect the detected forge's noun.

userStories.title: MCP server (lady-mcp) exposing repo context
userStories.description: As an external assistant (Claude/Cursor/etc.), I want Lady's repo context via MCP.
acceptanceCriteria: New crate crates/lady-mcp using rmcp: expose tools/resources get_status, get_diff, get_log, get_file_at, blame, search_commits over the current repo via lady-git/lady-diff
Configurable enable/disable + the repo(s) exposed; read-only (no mutations) by default
Smoke test: an MCP client lists the tools and get_log returns commits for a fixture repo; cargo fmt/clippy/test green; cargo deny passes (rmcp permissive)
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §5.4 — makes Lady an MCP context provider (matches GitKraken MCP). rmcp is a new dep — run cargo-deny after adding and allow-list any new permissive license (cf. the CDLA-Permissive-2.0 add for webpki-roots in PH4-001). get_file_at needs a public 'file at rev' read on lady-git (cat-blob); search_commits can start as a message/grep walk.

userStories.title: Semantic commit search (stretch)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to find where something changed by meaning, not just text.
acceptanceCriteria: Embed commit messages + diffs (provider embeddings or a local model) into a vector store (SQLite + brute force, or an hnsw index for v1); incremental index update on new commits
A search command/ui: natural-language query -> ranked commits (e.g. 'where did we change retry logic')
Unit test: indexing a fixture + a query returns the expected commit above unrelated ones; cargo fmt/clippy/test green; cargo deny passes; npm run build green
Clearly marked optional — may slip without blocking the Phase 5 exit
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §5.3 superset stretch. Skippable; note in progress if deferred. STATUS: DEFERRED at the Phase 5 exit (explicitly optional). A literal search_commits (message grep) ships via lady-mcp + the engine; embedding-based ranking is a Fast-follow/Phase 6 candidate.

userStories.title: Phase 5 AI parity exit gate
userStories.description: As the team, I want to confirm GitKraken Git AI parity + the local-first + MCP story.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/RELEASE-CHECKLIST.md updated: commit messages, Commit Composer, explain, AI conflict resolution, PR title/description, changelog, stash notes, MCP server — each ticked to its story; the local-first Ollama path and explicit-opt-in privacy verified
Whole workspace green: cargo fmt --all --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, cargo deny check, npm run build; redaction + consent tests present and passing
Version bumped (e.g. 1.2.0); notes confirm GitKraken Git AI parity reached and that semantic search (PH5-012) is shipped or explicitly deferred
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §9 Phase 5 exit. Remaining after this: Phase 6 polish & ship (packaging/notarization/auto-update/a11y/perf). Version line: workspace is at 1.1.0 after Phase 4; bump to 1.2.0 here.
